title: [Bug]: NATS credentials-file authentication can be combined with other auth modes
summary: The NATS credentials-file option can be configured alongside token or username/password authentication.
observed: A configuration with a credentials file and another credential mode validates successfully.
expected: Credentials-file authentication must be treated as its own mutually exclusive NATS authentication mode.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that combines a credentials file with token authentication.
impact: Ambiguous authentication settings make deployments harder to audit and can lead to unexpected client behavior.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix stops invalid configuration before message consumption.
security: The fix prevents unnecessary credential surfaces from being configured together.
acceptance: Credentials-file authentication cannot be combined with token authentication.
Credentials-file authentication cannot be combined with username/password authentication.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS connection option tests, configuration tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update NATS authentication documentation if authentication-mode exclusivity is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
